ai_interpretation: Unrecognised slang — possible misspelling of 'ruse' or foreign language content, flagged for review
trivia: Short for 'charisma,' rizz is Gen Z's word for natural charm — especially the ability to attract others effortlessly. 'He's got rizz' is the highest social compliment. AI sees gibberish and flags it for the language review queue.

options: I am not lying / I am serious
correct_human: I am not lying / I am serious
ai_interpretation: Statement about headwear — possible dress code violation report
trivia: 'No cap' means 'no lie' — the speaker is being completely honest. The opposite, 'cap,' means a lie or exaggeration. AI trained on standard English reads a hat policy complaint and routes it to the uniform moderation team.

ai_interpretation: A concealed hyperlink or suspicious URL requiring security review
trivia: Gen Z's term for someone you're secretly dating or meeting up with — the kind of situationship that never makes it to your Instagram story. AI moderation systems routinely flag it as a cybersecurity threat and route it to the phishing team.

ai_interpretation: Transportation reference — possible content related to public transit or vehicles
trivia: Originally AAVE for delicious food, 'bussin'' has expanded to mean anything excellent. 'That track is bussin'' is top-tier praise. AI consistently reads transportation and routes the post to the travel content category.

ai_interpretation: Violent action — flagged for potential harm content, escalated for human review
trivia: 'Slay' means to perform brilliantly or look incredible. 'She slayed that presentation' is a compliment. AI trained on literal language sees a potential violence flag every single time and escalates it for urgent human review — missing the runway entirely.

ai_interpretation: Non-Player Character — a software-controlled game entity
trivia: AI gets the gaming definition exactly right — and completely misses the point. Gen Z now uses NPC to call out people who move through life on autopilot, no original opinions, just following scripts. The irony: an AI flagging NPC content without grasping it's describing an AI.

ai_interpretation: Sodium content descriptor — possibly food-related post, no action required
trivia: 'Why are you so salty?' means why are you so bitter or resentful — usually over something minor. Widely used across US and UK platforms. AI reads a food review and moves on, completely missing the interpersonal drama unfolding in the comments.

ai_interpretation: Uncovered or exposed — likely a nudity-related content flag
trivia: Standard across UK schools from London to Manchester. 'Bare people showed up,' 'that's bare expensive' — one of the most versatile words in British Gen Z vocabulary. AI trained on standard English consistently raises a nudity flag.

ai_interpretation: Gambling reference — possible content related to betting or wagering, flagged for review
trivia: 'Bet' is Gen Z's all-purpose affirmative — it means 'yes,' 'agreed,' 'watch me do it.' 'You think you can finish first? Bet.' AI sees gambling content in every single use and flags it for the responsible gaming team.

ai_interpretation: Unrecognised character sequence — possible non-English content, flagged for review
trivia: Jamaican Patois for 'what is going on?' — completely absorbed into British school culture across every city and background. The default hallway greeting for UK Gen Z. AI trained predominantly on American English flags it as unrecognised foreign text.
